text: The surgeon uses a robotic needle driver to reconstruct the inner layer of the resection bed. A 3-0 monocryl suture on an SH needle is passed through the renal capsule into the defect, with the suture anchored by a knot and a hemlock clip on the renal capsule.

The surgeon uses a continuous suturing technique to close small vessels and repair entries into the collecting system within the resection bed. Care is taken to avoid placing sutures too deeply to prevent vascular injury.

The surgeon passes the needle from the inner layer to the outside of the kidney through the renal capsule, securing the stitch with a hemlock clip placed by the assistant. Additional sutures are placed to occlude small bleeding vessels, contributing to hemostasis during renoraphy.
